summary: I was born in California, but I didn't spend much time there as I was brought up in Tijuana shortly after. California represents the starting point of my life journey, even though most of my childhood memories are from across the border. It’s interesting how my life has been shaped by the proximity of California to Tijuana and San Diego.

Growing up in Tijuana gave me a unique perspective, especially since I was so close to San Diego. My family and I often crossed the border for work and fun, making Tijuana a central part of my identity. Even during difficult times, like the rise in violence in 2008, I felt a strong connection and unofficially became an ambassador for Tijuana in the Midwest through my academic and athletic achievements.

San Diego was always the other side of the border where we worked or had fun on weekends. The closeness of San Diego to Tijuana made it easy for my family and me to live a transborder lifestyle for many years. It was part of the dynamic environment that shaped my upbringing and family bonds.

The September 11 attacks in 2001 felt like a tsunami hitting our region. Before that, crossing the border between Tijuana and San Diego was easy, but after 9/11, the border became a massive wall. This event separated my family and me physically, turning a short commute into a five-hour ordeal and deeply affecting my college experience in the U.S.

I attended college and grad school in the United States, first in North Dakota and later pursuing graduate studies starting in 2008. Living and studying in the U.S. was challenging, especially after 9/11 when border crossing became difficult. Despite the struggles, the U.S. provided me with opportunities to grow academically and personally, and it’s where I met my husband and started building my family.

I went to a four-year institution in North Dakota for my undergraduate studies. Although I was physically there, mentally I struggled at first due to the border challenges and family separation after 9/11. By my second semester, I pushed myself out of my comfort zone and started to thrive, which was a turning point in my academic journey.
